body:
Deprecate the LspProgress autocommand event in favor of the newer, more generic, `Progress` event.

We need to find a way to do this that follows the deprecation policy.

comment_data.body: cc @mfussenegger @MariaSolOs @lewis6991 Does this make sense? Is there a use case for keeping `LspProgress` separate from `Progress`? If so, should we have the default `$/progress` handler fire both the `LspProgress` and `Progress` events?

In general would make sense, but afaik there's still no arbitrary `data` or `kind/type` (to filter -e.g. in case you only want to subscribe to lsp progress updates) type of field for `Progress` so if we replaced LspProgress as is we'd lose information if I'm not mistaken.

> Is there a performance penalty for storing the "kind/type" in the event data, rather than in the pattern you filter against?

Potentially. If not already, we may in the future choose to optimize pattern-based filtering. But that isn't necessarily relevant unless an event is very frequent/spammy.

What is currently blocking $/progress handler from directly calling nvim_echo? Is it you need some sort of source identifier? Or arbitrary data? On the original progress pr at some point there was a data field that could take in any arbitrary data but was dropped in simplification phase.

If it's just source we can add a source field in progress-data and filter events based on that instead of title. Or filter on a combined attribute like "source:title"

> Is it you need some sort of source identifier? Or arbitrary data?

Imho both would be ideal.

source/origin/kind to allow users to filter if they want to. (E.g. I would like to keep separating lsp progress from dap progress - although if we keep the `client.progress` property and `lsp.status()` that's also possible without adding a source identifier for nvim_echo, but wouldn't extend to other plugins)

And arbitrary data is needed to be able to carry on all the information the language server sends. In the specification the `ProgressParams` contains a `value` property typed as `LSPAny`, so the actual structure is up to the specific server.

nvim_echo can already have arbitrary data in progress. And this does get emitted through Progress event. Docs in progress-event should mention it. https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/c9e961994b16ed841be43541ef550bf3d3f043ec/runtime/doc/api.txt#L687-L688 So we just need to add a source field and filter on source:title. That should be enough right?

> So we just need to add a source field and filter on source:title. That should be enough right?

Not sure what you mean with filter on `source:title` - at least for the lsp case users probably won't know the titles's upfront. Are you referring to using that format for the pattern to support something like `lsp:*` ? If so - that's okay.
Yeah I was meaning `lsp:*`. With `source:title` one can filter events based of source or title or both.

> > Is it you need some sort of source identifier? Or arbitrary data?
> 
> Imho both would be ideal.

@mfussenegger do you mean both on the pattern (`source:title`), or set pattern to "source" and include `title` in the event-data ? the `source:title` pattern seems a bit quirky.

> do you mean both on the pattern

With both I was referring to

1. source filter 
2. arbitrary data payload.

For the `LspProgress` use-case I don't see `title` filtering via pattern as a requirement. (The `pattern` for LspProgress is actually the `kind` for the well-defined progress messages and is one of `nil`, `begin` or `end`)

Forgive me, but I'm a bit confused: How would that work in practice? Right now, these two events are at a very different "layer": `LspProgress` is triggered by an LSP notification, which I can add an autocommand for to `nvim_echo` a progress message, which triggers `Progress`, which (right now) triggers terminal progress events via OSC sequences. If `LspProgress` is replaced by `Progress`, wouldn't that be circular?

Or is the idea to completely deprecate `LspProgress` event-based handling in favor of writing a `$/progress` handler (which we semi-deprecated in favor of `LspProgress`...)

> Or is the idea to completely deprecate `LspProgress` event-based handling

Yes. Having 2 different Progress interfaces is not ideal.

Hmm, that's a bit of a step backwards then, but OK. But then the plan (and the title) should actually be "Deprecate LspProgress in favor of $/progress handlers" since the `Progress` event is (necessarily) lower-level and hence cannot be a replacement.

Elaborate? `nvim_echo` can set `data`, which can store LSP data in the `Progress` event. What problem is caused by directly emitting Progress events instead of LspProgress?

Subscribers can listen to `autocmd Progress lsp` (where `source='lsp'`).

I don't want to be difficult here, I am genuinely asking about what the plan is. Can you give me a concrete example of the code a user should replace the snippet in `:h LspProgress` with? (I understand enhancing `Progress` with richer data; I'm wondering how the "sandwich" would work if it is _also_ used in the default LSP progress handler.)
I'll need to think about this next week :)

> Can you give me a concrete example of the code a user should replace the snippet in `:h LspProgress` with?

I believe the idea is something along these lines:

1. Apply this diff:

    ```diff
    diff --git a/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/handlers.lua b/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/handlers.lua
    index f8c1db9607..ad0d1813dc 100644
    --- a/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/handlers.lua
    +++ b/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/handlers.lua
    @@ -78,11 +78,7 @@ RSC['$/progress'] = function(_, params, ctx)
     
       client.progress:push(params)
     
    -  api.nvim_exec_autocmds('LspProgress', {
    -    pattern = kind,
    -    modeline = false,
    -    data = { client_id = ctx.client_id, params = params },
    -  })
    +  api.nvim_echo({ ... }, false, { data = { ... }, id = ..., kind = 'progress', source = 'lsp' })
     end
     
     --- @see # https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specifications/specification-current/#window_workDoneProgress_create
    ```

2. Replace any `LspProgress *` autocommand with `Progress lsp` and appropriate callback adjustment to get the data. So like `autocmd Progress lsp redrawstatus`. The "Emit a |progress-message| on LSP progress events" would be redundant. Not sure how user's could opt-out of showing LSP progress it but still keeping progress for everything else.

> Not sure how user's could opt-out of showing LSP progress it but still keeping progress for everything else.

And what happens when `noice` etc. overrides the default handler. This doesn't replace the snippet, it removes the snippet, together with user customization. The `LspProgress` event was introduced precisely to avoid "fighting over" progress handlers.

> > Can you give me a concrete example of the code a user should replace the snippet in `:h LspProgress` with?
> 
> I believe the idea is something along these lines:
> 
>     1. Apply this diff:
>        diff --git a/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/handlers.lua b/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/handlers.lua
>        index f8c1db9607..ad0d1813dc 100644
>        --- a/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/handlers.lua
>        +++ b/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/handlers.lua
>        @@ -78,11 +78,7 @@ RSC['$/progress'] = function(_, params, ctx)
>         
>           client.progress:push(params)
>         
>        -  api.nvim_exec_autocmds('LspProgress', {
>        -    pattern = kind,
>        -    modeline = false,
>        -    data = { client_id = ctx.client_id, params = params },
>        -  })
>        +  api.nvim_echo({ ... }, false, { data = { ... }, id = ..., kind = 'progress', source = 'lsp' })
>         end
>         
>         --- @see # https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specifications/specification-current/#window_workDoneProgress_create
> 
>     2. Replace any `LspProgress *` autocommand with `Progress lsp` and appropriate callback adjustment to get the data. So like `autocmd Progress lsp redrawstatus`. The "Emit a |progress-message| on LSP progress events" would be redundant. Not sure how user's could opt-out of showing LSP progress it but still keeping progress for everything else.


This is basically what I had in mind. In the deprecation period we can emit both `LspProgress` event and `nvim_echo` and show a warning when someone registers a autocmd with LspProgress.

For 2 I was thinking eventually ui2 showing progress form Progress-event instead of msg_show event. Then there can be some source based filters there. 

Edit: Also for 2 progress message being shown in echo-area can be disabled with 'messageopt' then a Progress autocmd can be set that filters the progress messages based on source and shows only the ones user want to see. So this can already be customized from userend.

> And what happens when noice etc. overrides the default handler. This doesn't replace the snippet, it removes the snippet, together with user customization. The LspProgress event was introduced precisely to avoid "fighting over" progress handlers.

Listening to `Progress` is same as listening to `LspProgress` right? What does noice get extra by overriding the default handler?

labels.description: UI messages, log messages, dialogs, progress, errors, warnings, feedback
labels.name: messages

title: Deprecate LspProgress in favor of Progress

type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality
type.name: Enhancement
